The proposed alternative solution is to permit a single means of egress serving #rd floor and 2nd floor of a 3-storey residential building containing six (6) dwelling units by providing a fully automatic sprinkler system throughout the building in lieu of a second exit stairs. The Ontario Building Code requires two means of egress for a multi-unit residential building of this height. The proposed alternative solution replace the second means of egress with a fully automatic sprinkler system designed and installed in accordance with OBC Division B, Article 3.2.5 and NFPA 13.The building consists of three levels above grade and one level below grade with six dwelling units in total, a low occupant load, short and direct travel distances, and one enclosed exit stair. The sprinkler system will provide early detection and suppression of fire, significantly limiting fire growth, heat release, and smoke production.By controlling fire conditions at an early stage, the sprinkler system maintains tenable conditions within exit access routes and the exit stair for a sufficient duration to allow all occupants to safely evacuate using a single means of egress. The limited number of occupants reduced the likelihood of congestion during evacuation.The alternative solution satisfies the intent of the Ontario Building Code with respect to life safety and fire safety and achieves an equivalent or greater level of performance compared to the prescriptive requirements for a second means of egress.

City-initiated site plan application proposes a Community Recreation Centre adjacent to Sorauren Park. The community centre will be a 4 storey (26 metre) building with programs identified in the City's Parks and Recreation Facilities Master Plan to include a double gymnasium, an aquatic component, multipurpose rooms and fitness room spaces.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law amendment for 28-storey and 7-storey mixed-use buildings having a non-residential gross floor area of 883.9 square metres, and a residential gross floor area of 26017.0 square metres. 369 units are proposed, of which 76 are rental replacement units.

Site Plan Approval application to permit three new buildings on the lands, comprising a 47-storey mixed-use building with 5,770 square metres of non-residential uses in the lower floors and 620 dwelling units, a 21-storey building with 300 dwelling units, and a 9-storey building with 65 dwelling units, including 27 replacement rental units. A 2,209 square metre public park and enhanced open spaces with a range of programming across the site are proposed. A new public street, which would extend Rose Avenue through to the northern boundary of the site and a series of private streets across the site are proposed. The proposal includes modifications to the layout and uses within the existing buildings to create new amenity and non-residential spaces, which would result in the loss of 27 existing rental dwelling units that would be replaced in the new 9-storey building. The remaining 2,197 rental dwelling units are proposed to be retained, along with 2,986 square metres of existing non-residential space. The existing and proposed buildings would result in 3,182 dwelling units with a gross floor area of 224,675 square metres, and 8,756 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, for a total gross floor area of 233,431 square metres across the site.

To permit a mixed-use development including two tall buildings with heights of 29-storeys (approximately 99 m in height, measured to the top of the mechanical penthouse) connected by a 12-storey podium (approximately 41 m in height, measured to the top of the 12th floor). Overall, the site would include 592 residential units and 3,991 square metres of non-residential space. A total of 174 vehicle parking spaces and 463 bicycle parking spaces would be provided in a two level underground parking garage with the exception of 7 vehicle parking spaces which would be provided at-grade.

Site Plan Control application to permit a 38-storey mixed use building with a 7-storey podium, containing 421 residential units. Official Plan Amendment proposes a mixed-use development with four buildings with heights of 52, 41, 29, and 12 storeys. The proposed buildings will provide residential and commercial use at grade. The buildings will provide a total of 1245 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 1-bedroom + den, 2-bedroom, 2-bedroom + den and 3-bedroom.  A new public street will be provided, leading to  below-grade parking for the buildings. The proposed development provides a total GFA of 103,488.30 square metres and a FSI of 8.43.

Proposed mixed-use development containing two new buildings, including a 35-storey building in the southern portion of the site fronting on The Queensway (Building 1) and an inverted U-shaped building in the northern portion of the site which includes a 5- and 8-storey midrise elements, and a 20-storey tower element (Building 2). The proposed development includes a total gross floor area of 71,268 square metres. This includes 654 square metres of commercial GFA and 550 square metres of flexible community use space in Building 1, as well as 70,063 square metres of residential GFA spread between both buildings. A total of 649 residential units are proposed, including 154 rental replacement units, all owned and operated by TCHC, providing net new affordable and market housing.

The purpose of the Offical Plan and Zoning By-law amendment applications is to permit 4 residential and mixed use buildings with heights of 43, 31, 29, and 25 storeys. The applicant is proposing a total of 1,542 residential units and 1,902 parking spaces.  The applicant is proposing the development be built in 2 phases with phase 1 consisting of the 43 and 31 storey buildings and phase 2 consisting of the 29 and 25 storey buildings. This Official Plan Amendment application (file # 16 156153 NNY 24 OZ)  applies to all of the following properties 5925-5931,  5935A, 5945-5949, 5959, and 5995 Yonge Street, 11, 15, 17, 19, 21, 23, and 25 Wedgewood Drive, 1-8 Doverwood Court, and 42, 44, 46, 48, 50, 52, and 54 Cummer Avenue.  The properties subject to the zoning by-law amendment application (file # 16 121334 NNY 24 OZ) are 5945-5949 Yonge Street, 5959 Yonge Street, 1 & 2 Doverwood Court, and 48 Cummer Avenue.

A paved access from Wilket Road will be provided, leading to 1 level of below-grade parking, providing a total of 67 parking spaces; as well as 42 bicycle parking spaces. Rezoning application and district plan to permit a mixed-use development with a GFA of 705,280 square metres, including 635,780 square metres residential GFA (9,52 9 dwelling units) and 69,500 square metres non-residential GFA comprising 13,471 square metres retail, 24,505 square metres office and 31,524 square metres institutional with a density of 3.9 FSI and proposed building heights up to 63 storeys (195 metres). 10% of the residential GFA is proposed to be provided as affordable housing. The proposal includes five new local streets, two parks (total 16,300 square metres), and 4 privately-owned publicly accessible spaces. Proposed non-residential uses include a JK-Grade 8 TDSB school, district library, 3 childcare facilities, 929 square metres of community space, retail/commercial uses, a cultural destination, an office building, and a hotel.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment applications to permit three new buildings on the lands, comprising a 58-storey mixed-use condominium building with 4,060 square metres of non-residential uses in the lower floors and 741 dwelling units, a 21-storey purpose built rental building with 287 dwelling units with 310 square metres of community space, and a 9-storey purpose built rental building with 81 dwelling units. The proposal also includes 18 replacement rental units, of which the exact location remains to be determined.   A 3,135 square metre parkland conveyance plus a 772 square metre Stratified Public Park Area and enhanced open spaces with a range of programming across the site are proposed.    A new public street, which would extend Rose Avenue through to the northern boundary of the site and a series of private streets across the site are proposed.     The proposal includes modifications to the layout and uses within the existing buildings to create new amenity and non-residential spaces, which would result in the loss of 18 existing rental dwelling units that would be replaced elsewhere within the site as determined though the development review. The remaining 2,251 rental dwelling units are proposed to be retained, along with 1,828 square metres of existing non-residential space. The existing and proposed buildings would result in 3,360 dwelling units with a gross floor area of 231,028 square metres, and 6,198 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, for a total gross floor area of 237,226 square metres across the site.

The proposed building has an overall height of 68.4 metres (75.9 metres including mechanical penthouse), with a total gross floor area of 18,450 square metres.

The development would consist of a 27-storey building with 353 square metres of ground floor retail spaces and 314 residential dwelling units (158 1-bedrooms, 119 2-bedrooms, 37 3-bedrooms). Total Gross Floor Area (GFA) 21,913 m2 (21,560 m2 residential and 353 m2 retail) which results in a density of 8.0 times the lot area. 148 vehicular parking spaces and 230 bicycle parking spaces are provided.

Proposal for the redevelopment of the subject site with a 31-storey residential mixed-use building containing 408 residential units, approximately, 756.4 square metres of retail space is proposed on Floors 1 and 2, along with 27,094.9 square metres of residential space above.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment applications to permit three new buildings on the lands, comprising a 58-storey mixed-use condominium building with 4,060 square metres of non-residential uses in the lower floors and 741 dwelling units, a 21-storey purpose built rental building with 287 dwelling units with 310 square metres of community space, and a 9-storey purpose built rental building with 81 dwelling units. The proposal also includes 18 replacement rental units, of which the exact location remains to be determined.   A 3,135 square metre parkland conveyance plus a 772 square metre Stratified Public Park Area and enhanced open spaces with a range of programming across the site are proposed.    A new public street, which would extend Rose Avenue through to the northern boundary of the site and a series of private streets across the site are proposed.     The proposal includes modifications to the layout and uses within the existing buildings to create new amenity and non-residential spaces, which would result in the loss of 18 existing rental dwelling units that would be replaced elsewhere within the site as determined though the development review. The remaining 2,251 rental dwelling units are proposed to be retained, along with 1,828 square metres of existing non-residential space. The existing and proposed buildings would result in 3,360 dwelling units with a gross floor area of 231,028 square metres, and 6,198 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, for a total gross floor area of 237,226 square metres across the site.
The proposed development  is composed of two new residential towers located on a shared podium containing commercial, place of worship, and community uses.

The proposed development contemplates a one-storey commercial building containing a convenience eating establishment with a total GFA of 107.02 sq.m. Further, an associated drive thru (Tim Hortons) is proposed.

The application proposes six new towers and one new mid-rise ranging in height from 14-storeys to 34-storeys, with a total of 2,670 residential units and 1,000 square metres of at-grade retail space. A new L-shaped public road provides access to the site from Walsh Avenue in the north and from Wilson Avenue in the east. A private looped driveway extends from the public road to provide access to the site¿s underground parking entrances and at-grade pick-up and drop-off areas in the centre of the site.  A new public park is proposed on the eastern side of the site with frontage onto Walsh Avenue,  Wilson Avenue, and the new public road. A new outdoor amenity area is proposed in the centre of the site, and two mid-block connections with pedestrian access easements connect the centre of the site to adjacent public streets.

A Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit the construction of six residential towers in four separate buildings. The towers are proposed with heights ranging from 14 to 40 storeys and the podiums are proposed with heights ranging from six to 12 storeys. The application also proposes a public park at the corner of Walsh Avenue and Weston Road and a retail component along the Weston Road frontage.

Construct a 38 storey mixed-use building with retail at grade, residential above, and one level of underground parking. 382 units, approx gross floor area 22,576m2. Associated with 17 Locust Street.The first of the two development sites is located at the southeast corner of Weston Road and Locust Street (the West Site). The West Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 1175-1181 Weston Road and has a combined site area of approximately 1,143 square metres (0.114 hectares). The second of the two development sites is located further east of the West Site on the south side of Locust Street (the East Site). The East Site is comprised of the properties municipally known as 7-17 Locust Street and has a combined area of approximately 1,482 square metres (0.148 hectares). The West Site and the East Site are physically separated by a T shaped parcel of land known municipally as 1169 Weston Road that is owned and operated by the Toronto Parking Authority as a surface parking lot (the TPA Lands).

Site Plan Approval for the easterly block, known as Block 3, contains an L-shaped building with a 6-storey north-south element that connects to a 17-storey residential building. Site Plan Control Amendment. This application intends to formalize the current interim condition of the gas bar queue and adjacent parking area which have resolved circulation concerns raised by neighbouring property owners and the local Councillor. The proposed final condition will maintain the functional layoutof the interim condition while providing a more organized and aesthetically appealing separationof the gas bar queue from the adjacent parking area.
Proposed Development comprising of three (3) blocks of 4-storey stacked townhouses. The Proposed Development contains 125 dwelling units.

A request to amend the Official Plan and Zoning By-law to facilitate a mixed use development at 75-81 Billy Bishop Way (the "subject lands"). The proposal seeks to permit 5 towers, ranging in height from 21 to 49 storeys and containing 2,055 dwelling units on the subject lands. A total of 151,655 square metres of gross floor area ("GFA") is proposed, which includes 149,161 square metres of residential GFA and 2,493 square metres of non-residential GFA. In addition, a child care centre, privately-owned public space ("POPs"), 911 vehicular parking spaces and 1,603 bicycle parking spaces are proposed throughout the subject lands. Access to parking and loading areas will be facilitated through private laneways located throughout the site.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment applications to permit three new buildings on the lands, comprising a 58-storey mixed-use condominium building with 4,060 square metres of non-residential uses in the lower floors and 741 dwelling units, a 21-storey purpose built rental building with 287 dwelling units with 310 square metres of community space, and a 9-storey purpose built rental building with 81 dwelling units. The proposal also includes 18 replacement rental units, of which the exact location remains to be determined.   A 3,135 square metre parkland conveyance plus a 772 square metre Stratified Public Park Area and enhanced open spaces with a range of programming across the site are proposed.    A new public street, which would extend Rose Avenue through to the northern boundary of the site and a series of private streets across the site are proposed.     The proposal includes modifications to the layout and uses within the existing buildings to create new amenity and non-residential spaces, which would result in the loss of 18 existing rental dwelling units that would be replaced elsewhere within the site as determined though the development review. The remaining 2,251 rental dwelling units are proposed to be retained, along with 1,828 square metres of existing non-residential space. The existing and proposed buildings would result in 3,360 dwelling units with a gross floor area of 231,028 square metres, and 6,198 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, for a total gross floor area of 237,226 square metres across the site.

The revised (April 2020) Zoning Amendment Application for redeveloping the lands at 705 Warden Avenue comprises of  6, 18 and 19 storey buildings with a corresponding increase in residential gross floor area. The proposed redevelopment will result in a transit-oriented, mixed-use development comprising of the following:  -  44,575 square metres of residential gross floor area, a portion of which would be dedicated to affordable housing;  - 1,137 square metres of indoor and outdoor amenity space;  - 535 square metres of retail/commercial space; - 837 square metres of community space; and - 3,834 square metres of new public park space.

The site includes 470, 490 and 530 Wilson Avenue.  It is presently occupied by a four-storey commercial office building and two four-storey residential rental buildings that contain a total of 110 rental dwelling units. The applicant proposes to demolish the four-storey commercial office building and the two four-storey residential rental buildings and proposes to construct a 12-storey condominium building (394 units), and a 12-storey rental apartment building (161 units).  The condominium and apartment buildings will front onto and obtain access from Wilson Avenue.  The condominium and apartment buildings will include office replacement and retail space at grade along Wilson Avenue.  The proposed redevelopment also includes three levels of underground parking with a total of 387 spaces.  On October 30, 2018 the LPAT issued a decision approving the proposed development.  Refer to Case No. PL171059 for LPAT decision and conditions.
